Issue: Add explicit spatial_ndim tracking to MetaTensor to prevent dimension-mismatch crashes when einops.rearrange() or other reshape operations change ndim
Decision: Adds a `_spatial_ndim: int` attribute to `MetaTensor` that explicitly tracks the number of spatial dimensions
Rationale: Prevents dimension-mismatch crashes when `einops.rearrange()` or other reshape operations change `ndim`; propagates through `copy_meta_from` and is preserved through arbitrary torch operations; updates transforms to use `spatial_ndim` instead of hardcoded 3
Author: aymuos15
Description: MetaTensor class and related transforms in monai/data/meta_obj.py, meta_tensor.py, utils.py, and monai/transforms/
Cost: Moderate (involves changes to core classes and multiple transform files, but no new dependencies)
Risk: Potential side effects from incorrect spatial_ndim propagation or edge cases in batched operations (e.g., `(1,4,4)` affine matrices)

Argument.author: aymuos15
Argument.argument: Constructor uses `spatial_ndim = min(affine.shape[-1] - 1, ndim - 1)` — clamped by actual tensor dims to prevent out-of-bounds errors

Argument.author: aymuos15
Argument.argument: Affine setter uses `spatial_ndim = affine.shape[-1] - 1` — no clamping (user is explicit) to allow flexibility in affine transformations

Argument.author: aymuos15
Argument.argument: `peek_pending_affine` uses affine's inner matrix shape to fix batched `(1,4,4)` case, ensuring correct spatial_ndim inference

Argument.author: aymuos15
Argument.argument: `spatial_resample` uses `min(spatial_ndim, ndim - 1, 3)` — adds ndim-1 constraint as safety net to prevent invalid spatial dimensions
